rulings.Case Title: VICTOR TUNG VS. AMI QI et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY, MAUREEN DULLEA DEMURRER to 4TH Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Damages sufficiently alleged. Plaintiff has 10 days to answer upon entry of notice of order. Motion to strike is denied. Basis for recovery of attorney's fees sufficiently stated.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DICK MAK ET AL VS. ELDA AGAVE LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT ELDA AGAVE, LLC, JAY NATALE MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND Amended COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Plaintiff did not provide the Court with an adequate explanation addressing the inconsistent positions regarding the option taken in the UD action and this action, as previously addressed by the court. (Complaint,71.) Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. No opposition filed.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2023 line 4. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice. Proof of service on file indicates that the motion was served on March 7, 2023 via email and US mail, exactly 16 days before the hearing. Moving party failed to account for additional 5 calendar days for US mail service, or an additional 2 court days for e-service. Furthermore, Defendant did not file a stipulation permitting electronic service to Plaintiff in pro per. (CRC Rule 2.251(c)(3)(B).) Late-filed (6 court days before the hearing) and unauthorized first amended complaint is stricken.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DEBRA J DOLCH VS. JAMES M. SALINAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order Regarding Plaintiff'S Second Set Of Specially Prepared Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANT JAMES SALINAS Defendant'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order Regarding Plaintiff'S Second Set Of Specially Prepared Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant need not respond to Specially Prepared Interrogatories, Set Two. Plaintiff may not propound more than 35 interrogatories. Monetary sanctions are DENIED. (Code Civ. Proc., 2025.420(h).) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: BLUE POOL PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC. VS. TIM ELLIOTT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Deeming Admissions Admitted, Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions Against Tim Elliott; Memorandum In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Alexandra Azad
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2023 line 11. PLAINTIFF BLUE POOL PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Deeming Admissions Admitted, Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions Against Tim Elliott; Memorandum In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Alexandra Azad is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Requests for Admissions, Set One, are deemed admitted. Defendant is ordered to provide complete and verified responses without objections within 5 days of notice of entry of order to (1) General Form Interrogatories, Set One; (2) UD Form Interrogatories, Set One; (3) Special Interrogatories, Set One; and (4) Requests for Production of Documents, Set One. Monetary sanctions granted in the amount of $410.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
